title: Agricultural and fishing trades n.e.c.
description: Manage, improve, and protect natural resources to maximize their use without damaging the environment. May conduct soil surveys and develop plans to eliminate soil erosion or to protect rangelands. May instruct farmers, agricultural production managers, or ranchers in best ways to use crop rotation, contour plowing, or terracing to conserve soil and water; in the number and kind of livestock and forage plants best suited to particular ranges; and in range and farm improvements, such as fencing and reservoirs for stock watering. Research or study range land management practices to provide sustained production of forage, livestock, and wildlife. Plan, develop, and conduct programs to inform public of historical, natural, and scientific features of national, state, or local park. (Based on 40 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Provide information about and enforce regulations concerning environmental protection, resource utilization, fire safety, and accident prevention.
Plan, develop, and conduct programs to inform the public about natural and environmental features while managing forest protection activities, including fire control and public education programs.
Thin and space trees, control weeds and undergrowth, and perform animal care duties such as feeding, watering, and maintaining health records.
Conduct research or field experiments with plants, animals, soils, and diseases, including collecting and inspecting samples to diagnose health issues.
Supervise forestry operations, including nursery activities, timber harvesting, and land use practices such as grazing and disease control.
Maintain equipment, keep records, and administer emergency first aid when required, while also managing equipment inventories and issuing tools.
Evaluate and maintain the quality of logs and other forest products by assessing characteristics, grading, and recording data on the condition and volume.
Train and lead conservation workers or volunteers in activities related to managing natural resources, including forest or animal care duties.
Manage reforestation efforts, including site preparation, planting programs, and forest improvements to enhance land usage without damaging the environment.
Inspect and monitor activities related to wildlife management, such as hunting or trapping, and participate in related research activities.
Observe animals to detect signs of illness and provide treatment or advice on care and health management.
Organize and conduct community activities related to forestry and animal care to educate and promote awareness about these fields.
Develop computer databases and maintain information systems relevant to forestry and natural resources management.
Provide technical support to forestry research programs in areas such as tree improvement and experimental forestry.
Manage various operational logistics such as transporting logs, coordinating contractors, and maintaining safety rules.
Evaluate and determine strategies for tree felling, including assessing lean and planning cutting directions.
Train animals for various performance tasks, ensuring proper techniques based on breed characteristics and requirements.
Conduct and document soil and land assessments to identify suitable practices for sustainable agriculture and forestry.
Provide education and support to farmers and landowners demonstrating best practices in land and animal management.
Select, bait, and set traps, and lay poison along trails based on the species, size, habits, and environments of animals.
Track animals by checking for signs such as droppings or destruction of vegetation.
Obtain required approvals for using poisons or traps, and notify persons in areas where traps and poison are set.
Maintain and repair trapping equipment.
Remove catches from fishing equipment and measure them to ensure compliance with legal size.
Sort, pack, and store catch in holds with salt and ice.
Put fishing equipment into the water and anchor or tow equipment according to the fishing method used.
Monitor and adjust controls that regulate building systems such as air conditioning, furnace, or boiler.
Transport fish to processing plants or buyers.
Harvest marine life for human or animal consumption, using diving or dredging equipment, traps, or fishing rods.
Perform environmental sustainability assessments and develop plans for soil management or conservation practices to preserve rangelands and forests.
Provide information, knowledge, or expertise to land users regarding the conservation and management of natural resources.
Actively monitor environmental conditions and report on findings while providing guidance for restoration projects and environmental management.
Identify or recommend integrated weed and pest management strategies to help manage natural resources effectively.
Conduct environmental studies or conduct surveys related to wildlife habitat impact and other land use issues.
Assist in the development of educational programs about environmental practices for schools and community programs.
Coordinate with local and federal agencies to manage, protect, and improve rangelands and water resources.
Collect data on environmental impacts, including pollution and soil management to support conservation efforts.
Monitor projects during construction for compliance with design specifications and regulatory standards.
Examine local, regional, or global use and flow of natural resources to suggest conservation measures.
Develop and maintain relationships with local government staff to address environmental and land management issues.
Manage field offices or involve staff in cooperative ventures to promote sustainable land use practices.
Conduct public outreach to inform communities about conservation practices and environmental impacts.
Follow safety and maintenance protocols for all tools and equipment used in land and wildlife management activities.

Conduct studies and research on rangeland management practices and environmental effects of land and water use to provide sustained production of forage, livestock, and wildlife, while developing and communicating recommendations for landowners on maintaining or restoring environmental conditions.
Plan, develop, and implement programs or policies to improve environmental habitats, integrating conservation practices and ensuring compliance with regulations.

Irrigate soil using portable pipes or ditch systems, and maintain ditches, pipes, and pumps. Adjust, repair, and service farm machinery, and notify supervisors of any equipment malfunctions. Spray fertilizer or pesticide solutions to control pests and diseases using hand sprayers. Examine injured persons and administer first aid or CPR as necessary. Monitor and patrol recreational areas such as trails or swimming areas on foot or in vehicles. Contact emergency medical personnel in case of serious injury and provide emergency medical instructions to callers. Compile and present evidence for court actions, and investigate hunting accidents or reports of fish or game law violations.

Inspect recreational facilities and equipment for cleanliness and safety hazards, including inspecting commercial operations relating to fish or wildlife. Conduct surveys and compile figures of bag counts of hunters to determine the effectiveness of control measures. Collect and report information on populations or conditions of fish and wildlife, investigate crop, property, or habitat damage or destruction, and ensure compliance with laws regarding animal care.

Educate the public about wildlife conservation and regulations, and provide hunter or trapper safety training. Recommend methods to reduce potential financial fraud losses. Conduct inspections and clean facilities and equipment used for animal control.
Promote or implement wildlife management practices, design control measures to prevent or counteract damage caused by wildlife, and recommend revisions in hunting and trapping regulations.
Monitor and enforce laws regarding game and fish, patrol assigned areas to enforce regulations, and collaborate with local law enforcement agencies to report or investigate crimes.

Provide assistance with staff selection, training, and supervision. Participate in recreational demonstrations and organize games and recreational activities to promote physical, mental, and social development.

Conduct classes, workshops, and demonstrations, and provide individual instruction to teach topics and skills such as cooking, dancing, writing, and physical fitness. Plan class activities and manage classroom environments to facilitate learning. Adapt teaching methods and instructional materials to meet students' needs.
Prepare and maintain records, including logs of activities and student progress, and engage with other professionals to improve instructional programs.

Participate in search-and-rescue operations, provide assistance in safe use of recreational equipment, and engage in public safety efforts, including monitoring traffic and issuing warnings or citations.
Instruct participants in various recreational activities and provide safety precaution information, while monitoring performance and ensuring compliance with standards and regulations.

Train workers in techniques such as planting, harvesting, weeding, or insect identification, and in the use of safety measures. Also train workers in tree felling or bucking, operation of tractors or loading machines, yarding or loading techniques, or safety regulations.

Inspect crops, fields, or plant stock to determine conditions and need for cultivating, spraying, weeding, or harvesting, and inspect buildings, fences, fields or ranges, supplies, and equipment to determine work to be performed.
Monitor workers to ensure that safety regulations are followed, warning or disciplining those who violate safety regulations. Also, provide advice or information to park or reserve visitors.

Perform both supervisory and management functions, such as accounting, marketing, and personnel work, and confer with managers to determine production requirements, conditions of equipment and supplies, and work schedules.

Transport or arrange for transport of animals, equipment, food, animal feed, and other supplies to and from work sites, and coordinate the selection and movement of logs from storage areas, according to transportation schedules or production requirements.

Record the numbers and types of fish or shellfish reared, harvested, released, sold, and shipped, and observe fish and beds or ponds to detect diseases, monitor fish growth, determine quality of fish, or determine completeness of harvesting.

Monitor or oversee construction projects, such as horticultural buildings or irrigation systems, and prepare and maintain time or payroll reports, as well as details of personnel actions, such as performance evaluations, hires, promotions, or disciplinary actions.
